published_department: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

organizational_affiliations.label: Modern and Classical Languages and Literatures

biography: Laura Deiulio received her MA and PhD in Germanic Languages and Literatures from Princeton University. At Christopher Newport University, she teaches all levels of German classes as well as courses on literature in translation. Her research focuses on German Romanticism and women writers. She has published essays on Rahel Levin Varnhagen, Esther Gad, Lou Andreas-Salomé, and Anna Seghers, as well as the co-edited volume Gender, Collaboration and Authorship in German Culture: Literary Joint Ventures 1750-1850 (Bloomsbury, 2019). Currently, Laura is working on a book-length study of Rahel Levin Varnhagen’s thought.
education_entries: Ph D in Germanic Languages and Literatures, Princeton University
MA in Germanic Languages and Literatures, Princeton University
AB in Germanic Languages and Literatures, Washington University in St. Louis
teaching_interests: German language (all levels), German literature, German cultural history, advanced grammar and composition, the Enlightenment and Romanticism
publications: Book, Chapter in Scholarly Book-New (2021). "A Portrait of the Moment:" Rahel Levin Varnhagen's Letters at the Boundary of Life Writing . Camden House. Pages, 33-50.
